articles.title: Vision Banana — Perception Through an Invertible RGB Codec
articles.thesis: Vision Banana's codec isn't a convenience — it's the mechanism that routes around Zuo-2025's finding that Nano Banana Pro hallucinates plausible detail but loses on pixel-consistency metrics: generate RGB, invert the codec, measure meters instead of pixels.
articles.summary: Reads the paper against Wiedemer-2025 (Veo 3's emergent zero-shot vision) and Zuo-2025 (NBP's subjective-vs-pixel-metric dichotomy). Walks the power-transform + Gray-code Hamiltonian cube walk (Eq. 1, verified: each step flips one RGB bit); distinguishes VB's metric depth from Marigold's affine-invariant depth; re-tables the 6-specialist Depth Anything 3 comparison with the column mapping corrected (VB wins DIODE-Indoor by 0.079 over DA3, beats DA3 on nuScenes by 0.152 where DA3 collapses); names three mechanism claims (universal interface, mode-seeking, generalist-learners) and scores each against the evidence.

articles.title: LeWorldModel — Two Losses, One GPU, 48× Planning
articles.thesis: Move collapse prevention out of the architecture and into the objective — two losses, one GPU, and a world model a small team can actually train.
articles.summary: A JEPA-based world model that trains end-to-end from pixels with just two loss terms — L_pred and SIGReg. The real contribution isn't 15M-on-one-GPU; it's moving collapse prevention out of structural machinery (EMA teachers, frozen DINOv2, six-term losses) and into a single regularizer on the latent distribution.

articles.title: AlphaGo at 10 — The Loop That Shed Its Priors
articles.thesis: Move 37 was the spectacle; the mechanism was a closed loop of policy, value, MCTS and self-play — and the decade since has been a monotonic shedding of priors (human data → domain symmetries → rules → numerical verifier), with every 2026 frontier system running some version of that loop.
articles.summary: Ten years after Lee Sedol 4–1, re-read as mechanism. PUCT and the MCTS-as-policy-improvement-operator equation; the two 1-in-10,000 moves (37 and 78) as symmetric distribution-gap failures; the Fan → Lee → Master → Zero → AlphaZero → MuZero sequence as a catalogue of priors retired; the compute-inversion axis that separates AlphaGo's train-time search from o-series test-time search; and why Wang et al.'s 97%-beatable KataGo shows Move 78's problem is still the state of the art.

articles.title: Qwen3.5-Omni — ARIA and the Hybrid-Attention Thinker-Talker
articles.thesis: ARIA's one monotonic inequality replaces MFA and a fixed interleave constant. Hybrid-Attention is what makes 256k-ctx TTFC honest.
articles.summary: ARIA's one-line monotonic ratio replaces MFA + fixed interleave; Hybrid-Attention (GDN alongside attention) is the 3.5 move on top of 3-Omni's MoE. The release is API-only — a regression worth naming.

articles.title: On-Policy Distillation — The Reframe
articles.thesis: 'SFT vs RL' is the wrong axis. The right one is sampling-policy × reward-density — and on-policy distillation is the quadrant that was sitting empty.
articles.summary: A thoughts piece on Thinking Machines Lab's post. The 2×2 of sampling-policy × reward-density, the ~4-line incorporate_kl_penalty patch that fills the empty quadrant, the matched-LoRA 50–100× experiment, and why a frozen teacher makes this the only continual-learning primitive that doesn't drift.

articles.title: GLM-5 — DSA on an MLA Base, and Sequential RL That Doesn't Forget
articles.thesis: Three costs, all paid honestly: Muon Split kills logit blow-up, a 20B-token DSA retrofit replaces a 943B-token rewrite, and sequential RL stops forgetting by on-policy distillation across stages.
articles.summary: Three anti-cost patterns under one 744B/A40B roof: Muon Split kills logit blow-up without clipping, DSA retrofits onto MLA in 20B tokens (1/47 of V3.2), and on-policy cross-stage distillation stitches Reasoning→Agentic→General RL without forgetting.

articles.title: OneVL — When Latent Reasoning Meets the World Model
articles.thesis: Latent CoT finally beats explicit CoT — not because the latents got smarter, but because the decoder supervising them is the world, not the teacher.
articles.summary: First latent CoT to beat explicit CoT on driving — because the compression target moved from teacher text to future-frame tokens. Architecture, three-stage training, ablations, and the principle that travels.

articles.title: Kimi K2 — Why 85 on Creative Writing Isn't Luck
articles.thesis: The 85 on Creative Writing isn't luck — it's what falls out of a closed loop between verifiable reward and a self-critique rubric.
articles.summary: MuonClip stabilizes the 1T base; the real story is post-training — a 23k-tool agentic data factory plus a closed-loop self-critique rubric reward anchored on verifiable tasks.

articles.title: Kimi K2.5 — Dip-and-Recover: The Real Cost of Bolting Vision onto a 1T MoE
articles.thesis: A multimodal model isn't the text model plus a vision tower — it's the text model after 15T tokens of controlled damage, recovered by Joint RL and PARL.
articles.summary: From the 1T MoE base and the MoonViT-3D encoder, through Zero-Vision SFT, Joint RL and the PARL agent swarm — Kimi K2.5 taken apart layer by layer.
